fields.helpText: This is the unique message identifier from the eRx processing hub.
This is the message ID of any related or parent messages that may be  associated with this eRx Holding Queue message.
This is the date/time that the incoming eRx message was added to the eRx  Holding Queue file.
This is the external patient identifier.
This is the Vista linked patient. Pointer to file #2.
The institution associated with this eRx request. Pointer to the  INSTITUTION file (#4).
This is the pharmacy system associated with the incoming eRx message.
This is the message type associated with this incoming eRx request  (Change, Cancel, RxRenewal, Partial Fill, etc).
This is the external or provider order number associated with this  incoming eRx message.
This is the associated Vista order as stored in the PENDING OUTPATIENT  ORDERS file (#52.41).
This is the OE/RR order number from the ORDERS file (#100) associated  with this incoming eRx message.
This is the prescription file entry (#52) associated with this incoming  eRx message.
This is the eRx processing hub ID that is related to this message.
This is the VistA Rx record used to fill the data related to the incoming Drug, Dosage, SIG, Days Supply, Quantity and # of Refills.
This is the status for the incoming eRx message.
This is the person who manually validated the drug.
The date/time of the manually validation of the drug for the eRx.
This is the person who manually evaluated the patient for the incoming  eRx.
The date/time the patient was manually validated.
This is the auto-matching status for a provider associated with the  incoming eRx prescription request.
This is the manual validation status of the provider associated with the  incoming eRx prescription request.
This is the drug auto-matching status for the drug associated with this  incoming eRx prescription request.
This is the manual validation status for the drug associated with the  incoming eRx prescription request.
This is the drug auto-matching status for the patient associated with this incoming eRx prescription request.
This is the manual validation status for the patient associated with the  incoming eRx prescription.
This is the person who manually validated the provider for the eRx.
This is the date/time the provider was validated.
The external provider for this incoming prescription.
This is the external pharmacist associated with the incoming prescription  message.
The VA (matched) provider. This is the provider found in the NEW PERSON  file which matches the incoming provider information associated with the  eRx message.
This is the to/from qualifier for the incoming eRx message.
This is the external pharmacy associated with the incoming eRx.
This is the supervisor associated with the incoming eRx prescription.
This field identifies whether the VistA Provider was auto-validated or  not.
This is the incoming drug/supply item for the eRx holding queue entry.
This is the VA matched drug from the DRUG file (#50), that is the  equivalent to the incoming external drug/supply item passed in.
This is the incoming product code.
The drug db code qualifier for the incoming eRx.
This is the product code qualifier associated with the product code.
This is the strength for the drug/supply item.
This is the drug db code for the incoming eRx.

fields.setOfCodes: AA:NCI PHARMACEUTICAL DOSAGE FORM;AB:NCI UNITS OF PRESENTATION;AC:NCI POTENCY UNIT;
fields.helpText: This is the form source code from the external source for the incoming  eRx prescription.
This is the incoming form code value from the external source for the  incoming eRx prescription.

fields.setOfCodes: AA:NCI PHARMACEUTICAL DOSAGE FORM;AB:NCI UNITS OF PRESENTATION;AC:NCI POTENCY UNIT;
fields.helpText: The strength source code for the incoming eRx drug or supply item.
The strength code associated with the eRx drug/supply item.
This is the schedule for the eRx incoming prescription (drug/supply item).
This is the quantity for the drug/supply item.
The code list qualifier for the incoming eRx drug/supply item.

fields.setOfCodes: AA:NCI PHARMACEUTICAL DOSAGE FORM;AB:NCI UNITS OF PRESENTATION;AC:NCI VALUES OF PROPERTY OR ;AC:NCI POTENCY UNIT;
fields.helpText: The unit source value for the incoming eRx drug/supply item.
The potency unit code for the drug/supply item.
The days supply for the incoming eRx drug or supply item.
This is the number of refills for the incoming eRx prescription.

fields.setOfCodes: R:NUMBER OF REFILLS;PRN:AS NEEDED;P:PHARMACY REQUESTED REFILLS;A:ADDITIONAL REFILLS AUTHORIZED;
fields.helpText: This is the refill qualifier associated with the eRx incoming  prescription.

fields.setOfCodes: 0:NO PRODUCT SELECTION INDICATED;1:SUBSTITUTION NOT ALLOWED BY PRESCRIBER;
fields.helpText: Substitution definition, as needed. This holds the information regarding  substitutions for a drug/supply item.
This is the date that the eRx incoming prescription was written by the  provider.
This is the last fill date of the eRx prescription.
The eRx prescription expiration date.
The effective date of the eRx prescription.
The period end for the incoming eRx prescription.
This is the delivery date of the eRx prescription.
This is the validation date of the eRx prescription.
This is the date until when the eRx should be held for a future fill.
The directions for the eRx prescription.
The notes associated with the eRx prescription.
The diagnosis values associated with the eRx patient/prescription.
The prior authorization value for the eRx prescription.
This is the prior authorization qualifier for the eRx prescription.
The prior authorization status for the eRx authorization.

1 - Yes - Used for medications ordered by a prescriber not requiring  dispensing, but available for reference and drug-to-drug interaction  checking. 2 - Emergency - Indicates that the prescription should not be filled.  This is a cover prescription for a previously called in emergency oral  prescription as necessary for a controlled substance. 3 - Hold - Used for medications ordered by a prescriber but not requiring  dispensing at this time.
This is the date/time that the prescription is needed by.
This is the time zone associated with the needed by date/time.
This is the time zone difference quantity related to the needed by time zone value.
Enter the reason for the needed by date/time.
This sub-file holds the structured SIG information from the incoming XML  message.
This multiple contains the order check information.
This the patient location facility unit associated with the incoming eRx  prescription.
The physical bed that the patient is occupying.
The room where the patient is physically located.
This is the observation multiple that holds all observations received for  an eRx prescription.
The observation notes associated with the eRx prescription.
This sub-file holds the drug use evaluation information for an incoming  eRx prescription.
This is the external pharmacy that sent the eRx prescription to the VA.
This is the external pharmacist associated with the incoming eRx  prescription.
The external VA institution where the prescription was transferred.
This is the external pharmacy to where the eRx prescription was  transferred.
The payer/cardholder information for the associated eRx prescription.
This multiple tracks the status changes that have occurred for an eRx.
This multiple works as an audit log to capture data related to changes  made to VistA Patient, Provider and Drug/Sig in a Holding Queue record,  either by auto-matching or manually.
This is the Vista quantity for the eRx prescription. This is the quantity  needed for a prescription in Vista.
This is the VistA Days supply value associated with the incoming eRx.
This is the medication instruction (verb) for the eRx.

This is the pickup routing method for the incoming eRx. Available options are W (WINDOW) or M (Mail) if the eRx is going to be dispensed or P (PARKED) if the eRx is not supposed to be dispensed until requested.
The number of vista refills for this incoming eRx.
The vista clinic associated with the incoming eRx.
This is the quantity/timing sub-file which holds information about dosing  for a medication.
The FROM value as passed from the incoming XML.
The from qualifier attribute associated with this eRx.
This is the 'To' value as passed in as part of the header portion of the  eRx XML message.
This is the 'To' qualifier attribute that is passed within the incoming  XML. This To qualifier is found in the 'Header' parent of the XML.
This is the date time the eRx was received by the vista system.
This is the institution that the incoming eRx belongs to.
This is the medical center division associated to the incoming eRx.
This is the senders secondary identification value.
This is the senders tertiary identification value.
This is the receiver secondary identification value.
This is the receiver tertiary identification.
This is the Change Healthcare message identifier. This number is not to  be considered to be a unique identifier. It is possible that the same  message ID could come from more than one provider.
This is the pending outpatient order in file 52.41, once the eRx has been  sent to the file.
This field holds the VA SIG information, which will be sent to pending  outpatient orders as the SIG.

This field holds the patient instructions for an eRx. This field will be  transferred to the pending outpatient orders file upon acceptance of an  eRx. This field is the equivalent to the patient instructions field in  the pending outpatient orders file.
The drug coverage status multiple to hold the drug coverage status values.
This field contains the Indication For Use for this eRx medication.
This field determines whether or not to include the Indication For Use in the SIG. 1 - to include in the SIG ; 0 - not to include
This field contains the Other Language Indication For Use for this eRx  medication.
This is the VA provider comments. This represents the provider comments  for a prescription, which is the VA's version of the 'DIRECTIONS' that  are received with an eRx.
The un-expanded SIG for the eRx prescription.
The external form code for the medication being prescribed.
This is the external form of the potency unit code.
This is the external value of the strength code.

If this flag is set to yes, the payer cardholder ID values have been  converted into the new format. This needs to be set if the conversion has  taken place so no futher conversion attempts will occur on this record.  The cardholder id field is not a required field on an incoming eRx and  was previously being used as the .01 field. This has been changed to now  be a sequence value, and cardholder ID has been moved into field #7.
This multiple holds the medication dispensed/medication(s) requested in  support of the refill request and change request message types.
This is the refill request/response comments for the eRx refill request  or refill response.
This is the person who entered the note related to an eRx refill request  or response.
This is the date/time the note was entered for a request or response type  of message.
This is the person who made the outbound refill/change request.
This is the number of refills requested on an prescription refill request.

fields.setOfCodes: A:APPROVED;D:DENIED;DNP:DENIED, NEW RX TO FOLLOW;AWC:APPROVED WITH CHANGES;R:REPLACE;V:VALIDATED;
fields.helpText: This is the response type for a refill request. This tells us the status  of the request, as returned by the provider.
This is the note as written by the provider, for a refill or cancel  response message type.
This is the reference number associated with the refill or change request  response.
This multiple holds the response codes related to the refill or change  response.
This is the error text for an inbound or outbound eRx error message.

fields.setOfCodes: 600:COMMUNICATION PROBLEM - TRY AGAIN LATER;601:RECEIVER UNABLE TO PROCESS - DO NOT RETRY;602:RECEIVER SYSTEM ERROR - TRY AGAIN LATER;900:TRANSACTION REJECTED - DO NOT RETRY;
fields.helpText: This is the error code associated with an eRx error message.
This multiple holds the error description codes for a request/response  message.
This is the facility name associated with the incoming eRx.
This is the address line 1 associated with the facility.
This is the address line 2 associated with the facility.
This is the city associated with the facility.
This is the state associated with the facility.
This is the zip code associated with the facility.
This is the country code associated with the facility.
This multiple contains information related to the eRx facility.
This multiple contains information related to the eRx facility.
This subfile contains the facility communication information associated  with an eRx. This subfile was created to support the 2017 script standard.
This is the NCPDPID associated with the facility identification. This  field was created to support the 2017 script standard.
This is the state license number associated with the facility  identification. This field was created to support the 2017 script  standard.
This is the Medicare Number associated with the facility identification.  This field was created to support the 2017 script standard.
This is the Medicaid Number associated with the facility identification.  This field was created to support the 2017 script standard.
This is the UPIN associated with the facility identification. This field  was created to support the 2017 script standard.
This is the facility identification. This field was created to support  the 2017 script standard.
This is the Drug Enforcement Agency number associated with the facility  identification. This field was created to support the 2017 script  standard.
This is the HIN associated with the facility identification. This field  was created to support the 2017 script standard.
This is the NPI associated with the facility identification. This field  was created to support the 2017 script standard.
This is the mutually defined value associated with the facility  identification. This field was created to support the 2017 script  standard.
This is the REMS healthcare settings enrollment identification associated  with the facility identification. This field was created to support the  2017 script standard.
This is the facility direct address associated with the eRx. This field  was created to support the 2017 script standard.

This field identifies the change request type for cancel and change eRx  messages.   C1 Significant change (Any changes to the Drug, form, strength, dosage,  or route)   C2 Frequency Change (Any change to the frequency or hours of  administration for the drug)   C3 Insignificant Change (All other changes)
This is the return receipt associated with a cancel or change eRx message.
This is the request reference number for a cancel or change eRx message.
This is the change of prescription status flag, intended to be used for  cancel and change eRx message types.
This flag identifies entries that have been denied by the eRx processing  hub.
This field indicates if the incoming eRx prescription is for a controlled  substance.
This is the encryption hashing  method (e.g., SHA-1, SHA-256, etc.) used to encode the digital  signature.
This value is used to confirm that all fields have been included in the  digital signature.
This is the digest value that has been signed with a private key.
This is a string of the base64 encoded raw bytes of the X509 certificate  (which contains the Public Key), used by the receiver to validate the  digital signature.
This sub-file contains the processing errors associated with the eRx entry.
This is the LTC level of change associated with the incoming eRx. This  field is being created to support the 2017 script standard.
This is the urgency indicator code associated with the incoming eRx. This  field is being created to support the 2017 script standard.
This is the prohibit renewal request associated with the incoming eRx.  This field is being created to support the 2017 script standard.
This is the no known allergies field associated with an incoming eRx.  This field is being created to support the 2017 script standard.
This subfile contains all information relating to allergies of an  incoming eRx. This subfile is being created to support the 2017 script  standard.
This subfile contains all information relating to the benefits  coordination section of an incoming eRx. This subfile was created to  support the 2017 script standard.
This is the observation notes associated with the incoming eRx. This  field was created to support the 2017 script standard.
This subfile contains the observation section of the incoming eRx. This  subfile was created to support the 2017 script standard.
This is the follow-up prescriber related to the incoming eRx. This field  was created to support the 2017 script standard.
This subfile holds the medication details for an eRx. This can be the  prescribed, requested, or dispensed medication.
This is the version number associated with the incoming eRx. If set to 1,  version is confirmed to be 20170715, if 0, field will be set to null.
This is the request reference number associated with the eRx.
This is the return receipt associated with the eRx.
This is the ECL version number associated with the eRx.
This is the data type version associated with the eRx.
This is the structures version associated with the eRx.
This is the transaction domain associated with the eRx.
This is the transaction version associated with the eRx.
This is the transport version associated with the eRx.
This is the sender software developer associated with the eRx.
This is the sender software product associated with the eRx.
This is the sender software version release associated with the eRx.
This is the message request code associated with the change  request/response.
This is the message request sub code associated with the change  request/response.
This is the Rx Change Request Reason Text that was send to the outside prescriber.
This is the state license number associated with the change response.
This is the medicare number associated with the change response.
This is the medicaid number associated with the change response.
This is the UPIN associated with the change response.
This is the DEA number associated with the change response.
This is the HIN associated with the change response.
This is the social security number associated with the change response.
This is the NPI associated with the change response.
This is the reference number returned by TIU when a Patient Progress Note  is automatically created when the user submits a Change Request to  the ouside Prescriber.
This is the Change Request comment for the Patient Progress Note that is  automatically created when the Change Request is submitted to the outside  prescriber.
This is the certificate to prescribe associated with the change response.
This is the data 2000 waiver identification associated with the change  response.
This is the mutually defined information associated with the change  response.
This is the REMS healthcare provider enrollment identification associated  with the change response.
This is the state control substance number associated with the change  response.
This is the supervisor associated with the change response.
This is the date associated with the change response. This field only  applies to validated types of change responses.
This is the specialty associated with the change response.
